summary: All round-1 major findings (T6-R1-01, T6-R1-02) are resolved correctly and the scrubSecrets cycle fix (T6-R1-06) is a genuine improvement over the original. The WeakMap-based secret storage is strictly stronger than the private class field approach — no class member can extract the key. No new issues introduced.

round1_resolution.notes: getRelayerKeySecret removed from index.ts re-exports. A clear comment explains why. settle.ts can import it directly from ./relayer-key.js. Verified: grep finds only the explanatory comment, not an export statement.

Rather than making _extract a private class method (#extract), the implementation replaced the entire class-field approach with a module-private WeakMap<OpaqueRelayerKey, string>. This is strictly stronger: there is NO class member (public, private, or otherwise) that can extract the key. getRelayerKeySecret is the only extraction path and it is not re-exported from index.ts. The defensive TypeError on SECRET_TABLE.get() === undefined correctly guards against prototype-manipulation attacks.
UP_SUPPRESS_T3_NOTES=1 prefixed to both test and test:e2e scripts in package.json with an explanatory //test-env comment. Notes still fire in non-test contexts as intended.

insert() now has a JSDoc explaining it does not lazy-evict (no now arg) and steering production callers to checkAndInsert. This is the correct trade-off — adding now to InsertInput would change the public API. The replay-store test also gains size() assertions around the TTL eviction test, making the stale-count behavior explicitly observable.

scrubSecrets was rewritten from WeakSet-based cycle detection (returns original on re-visit) to Map<original, scrubbed_copy>-based tracking, pre-registering each output before walking children. This correctly resolves back-edges to the partial scrubbed copy. Three new tests verify: (1) cycle back-edge points to scrubbed copy (out.self === out, out.self.v contains REDACTED), (2) array cycles, (3) DAG shared-reference preservation (out.a === out.b). Logic verified by manual trace and node -e confirmation.
Dead static readonly [BRAND] field removed from OpaqueRelayerKey class. Instance brand stamp in constructor is the only brand mechanism, consistent with is() checking the instance property.
Leading zeros in PRICE_RE not tightened (e.g. '00.01'). The math is correct; this is a cosmetic edge case not covered by the spec. Acceptable to defer.

positive_observations: WeakMap approach is a stronger isolation guarantee than private class fields: not even subclass authors or prototype manipulators can get the key out via any class member path.
scrubSecrets Map-based cycle resolution correctly handles the leaking-back-edge case AND preserves shared DAG references — both properties are now tested.
The parseUsdPrice zero-reason test was simplified with toThrowError(expect.objectContaining({reason: 'zero'})) — cleaner than the try/catch approach it replaced.
Adding the missing 'payload' leaf to the decodeXPayment table-driven test fills a gap from round 1 (11 leaves were listed in the spec; the test now covers all of them).
